items.title: KV-cache in LLM inference
items.scenario: An LLM serves long conversations; recomputing K/V matrices for the full prompt each token would dominate latency.
items.explanation: Cache K/V per layer per token (lots of memory, often GB for long contexts) to skip the recompute (huge time saving). Classic 'pay memory, save time'.

items.title: Vector index for retrieval
items.scenario: Find the top-k nearest embeddings from 100M vectors in under 50 ms.
items.explanation: Brute scan is O(n) — too slow at 100M. HNSW or IVF indexes cost GB of memory but bring query latency down to O(log n)-ish.

items.title: Prompt caching at the API layer
items.scenario: Many requests share the same long system prompt; recomputing it for each call wastes compute.
items.explanation: Cache the prefix's tokenization + first-pass attention; pays storage to skip the redundant compute.

items.title: Quantization (int8 / int4)
items.scenario: A 70B model in fp16 needs 140GB of VRAM, doesn't fit on a single accelerator.
items.explanation: Quantize weights to 4 bits → ~35GB. Pays a small loss of quality (and some compute for dequantization) for a massive memory saving.

items.title: Streaming generation
items.scenario: Generating a 10k-token response — buffering the whole thing balloons RAM and TTFB.
items.explanation: Stream tokens as they're produced — O(1) memory in the buffer, at the cost of more orchestration code (back-pressure, partial parsing).

items.title: Rolling rows in edit-distance DP
items.scenario: Eval pipeline computes edit distance on 100k pairs of long strings.
items.explanation: The full m × n DP table is O(m·n) memory; keeping only the previous row reduces it to O(n) with identical time.
